title: Filtering, Three-Valued Logic, Patterns, Ordering, and Pagination · Result-Grain Case: Where Predicates
summary: A queue view must include the correct unresolved tickets, handle missing owners, and remain deterministic across pages. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql where predicates increment in a deterministic queue-filter query suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Combine comparison, range, membership, and boolean predicates with explicit grouping.
Use duplicate-preserving and duplicate-eliminating results intentionally and explain their cost and semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Return unresolved high-or-critical tickets plus unowned work from the seeded database. Expose ticket_id, a readable owner label, age bucket 2, and deterministic severity/opened ordering; then test a NULL owner and tied timestamp. Competency probe: Combine comparison, range, membership, and boolean predicates with explicit grouping. A defensible solution applies Where Predicates, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a deterministic queue-filter query suite.

content.code:
-- Worked focus: sql-where-predicates — Combine comparison, range, membership, and boolean predicates with explicit grouping.
SELECT ticket_id,
       COALESCE(CAST(owner_id AS TEXT), 'unassigned') AS owner,
       CASE severity WHEN 'critical' THEN 1 WHEN 'high' THEN 2 ELSE 3 END AS severity_order
FROM tickets
WHERE resolved_at IS NULL
ORDER BY severity_order, opened_at, ticket_id
LIMIT 7;

-- Evidence: sql-where-predicates-1si51j

FROM tickets
WHERE title LIKE '%i%'
ORDER BY opened_at, ticket_id
LIMIT 4;
